title: vicia cracca subsp. kitaibeliana
intro: Vicia cracca subsp. kitaibeliana, also known as the Hungarian vetch, is a subspecies of perennial legume plant native to Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Vicia cracca subsp. kitaibeliana include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
